key_insight: Twisting multilayer H-MoS2 breaks symmetry and activates new shift-current channels, making the photocurrent response tunable by twist angle.
engineering_relevance: This suggests moiré engineering can be used to design low-dimensional optoelectronic materials with controllable nonlinear photovoltaic output beyond conventional p–n junctions.
method: Built a Slater-Koster tight-binding model for large moiré supercells, parametrized from first-principles.
Compared tight-binding results against density functional theory and Wannier-interpolated calculations.
Analyzed band-edge electronic structure, conductivity spectra, and shift distance versus twist angle and layer number.
evidence_signal: The model reproduces the electronic structure near the band edges and the main spectral features of the shift-current conductivity across mono-, bi-, and trilayer and twisted moiré cases.
watch_item: Accuracy is strongest near the band edges, so results for higher-energy states or stronger correlation effects may require further first-principles validation.
paper_title: Twistronic control of shift current in multilayer moiré system
